statement: Current operator surfaces conflate historical failed attempts with terminal target failure and are insufficient for post-hoc parse forensics.
rationale: Clean completed runs still show failed requests and error summaries, while failed parse requests usually lack raw output on durable request rows and local mirrored telemetry drops payload_json.
keyAssumptions: Operators need both terminal truth and historical attempt history, but they need them separated.
validationCriteria: A clean completed run should be representable as terminally clean while still exposing historical attempts separately.
A failed parse request should retain enough durable payload to reconstruct the failure without relying solely on Axiom.
critiquePoints: Some historical-failure visibility may be intentional for forensic uses.
Retention limits may constrain how much local payload can be preserved.
questions: Which surface should own terminal truth vs historical-attempt visibility?
